title: My Five Senses Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To use your eyes to look at something.
rounds.explanation: see: To use your eyes to look at something.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The two parts at the end of your arms.
rounds.explanation: hands: The two parts at the end of your arms.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Something flat is smooth with no bumps or hills.
rounds.explanation: flat: Something flat is smooth with no bumps or hills.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To use your ears to know a sound.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The five thin parts on each of your hands.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Something short is not very long or tall.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I can see the moon tonight.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My eyes help me read my book.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I wash my hands before eating.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which word tells how honey tastes?
rounds.explanation: Honey tastes sweet; noisy is for sounds, shiny is for looks and rough is for touch.

rounds.prompt: Which of these can you hear?
rounds.explanation: A ringing bell makes a sound; the flower is for looking, the blanket for touching and the date for tasting.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence about our senses is true?
choices: I can see with my eyes.
I can hear with my nose.
I can see with my ears.
I can smell with my hands.
rounds.answer: I can see with my eyes.
rounds.explanation: Eyes are for seeing; ears hear, the nose smells and hands touch, so the other sentences are mixed up.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: Who took Amal to the busy market?
choices: To give thanks for their wonderful senses
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Her grandmother, Selma

rounds.prompt: What was the man calling out in the market?
choices: I can hear with my ears.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: 'Fresh fish! Fresh fish!'

rounds.prompt: Why did Selma say 'Alhamdulillah' at the end?
choices: I can see with my eyes.
To give thanks for their wonderful senses
rounds.answer: To give thanks for their wonderful senses
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: To give thanks for their wonderful senses

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
rounds.target: Point to the body part an adult names, then say the sentence back.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
rounds.target: Feel something hidden in a bag and describe it.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 - Role-play
rounds.target: Play a body-part game and point as you speak.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The library is nice and quiet.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The table top is very flat.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The tractor is louder than the car.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'soft' mean in this unit?
choices: Something soft feels gentle and nice to touch.
To use your eyes to look at something.
To use your nose to find out what something is like.
rounds.answer: Something soft feels gentle and nice to touch.
rounds.explanation: soft: Something soft feels gentle and nice to touch.

rounds.prompt: What does 'long' mean in this unit?
choices: To use your eyes to look at something.
To use your nose to find out what something is like.
Something long goes a big way from end to end.
rounds.answer: Something long goes a big way from end to end.
rounds.explanation: long: Something long goes a big way from end to end.

rounds.prompt: What does 'smell' mean in this unit?
choices: The two parts of your face that help you see.
To use your nose to find out what something is like.
To use your eyes to look at something.
rounds.answer: To use your nose to find out what something is like.
rounds.explanation: smell: To use your nose to find out what something is like.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'cold'?
rounds.explanation: cold is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'sweeter'?
rounds.explanation: sweeter is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'nose'?
rounds.explanation: nose is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: To use your nose to find out what something is like.
The two parts of your face that help you see.
The soft pink part inside your mouth that tastes food.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: Something soft feels gentle and nice to touch.
A quiet sound is very small and soft.
Something juicy is full of juice that wets your fingers.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Something long goes a big way from end to end.
Making a bigger sound than another person or thing.
Feeling more cold than another thing you touch.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which sense do you use with your tongue?
rounds.explanation: The tongue tastes food; seeing, hearing and smelling use the eyes, ears and nose.

rounds.prompt: Which word names one of your five senses?
rounds.explanation: Touch is a sense; jump is an action, a pencil is a thing and Monday is a day.

rounds.prompt: Aisha holds a piece of ice. How does it feel?
rounds.explanation: Ice feels cold when you touch it; loud is a sound, bright is a sight and sweet is a taste.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which body part do you use to see?
rounds.explanation: We see with our eyes; ears are for hearing, the nose is for smelling and hands are for touching.

rounds.prompt: Which body part do you use to hear?
rounds.explanation: We hear with our ears; eyes are for seeing, the tongue is for tasting and feet are for walking.

rounds.prompt: Which body part do you use to smell a flower?
rounds.explanation: We smell with our nose; hands touch, ears hear and eyes see.
